# How does xray-connector work

## Integration with Xray

### Xray API

xray-connector uses Xray REST API to synchronise test cases from JIRA/Xray to the test automation framework and publish the tests results back to JIRA/Xray.

It sits on your test automation framework’s side and acts as a client of JIRA/Xray API.

### Xray test objects

To identify which tests should be retrieved from Xray, xray-connector uses a Xray **Test Plan** id. The corresponding Xray test plan should exist in your JIRA project and all tests that you want to be retrieved by the test automation framework should be associated to it.

The tests in this test plan should be declared as **Cucumber** tests in JIRA/Xray to be considered as automated tests.

### Feature steps implementation

In order to execute the tests retrieved from Xray as feature files, your Test Automation Framework will need to know how to implement the steps used in the feature files.

Depending on the framework, this may require for you to implement those steps in the Test Automation Framework (for instance, Cypress). Some frameworks, though, will spare you this effort as they already have built-in steps implementation available. Karate is an example of such a framework.

## Feature files retrieval

xray-connector can retrieve feature files generated by Xray.

## Test plan file generation

xray-connector can retrieve test plan description, and parse it to create a file in JSON format used to specify your gatling tests.

## Test execution reports post processing

Test execution reports created by Test Automation Frameworks are not always compatible with Xray.

This is why xray-connector provides commands to process these reports and prepare them for being published in Xray.

As of now, two test execution reports processing features are available in xray-connector:
* merging separated JSON Cucumber execution reports into a single reports (required by Cucumber and Cypress)
* converting a Gatling execution report into an Xray compatible report (required by Gatling)
